evals.prompt: Please peer-review my manuscript. Run /peer-review --file evals/fixtures/minimal.tex
evals.expected_output: The skill checks for the review/addition-suggestion environments (they are absent), shows a preamble patch and asks for approval, extracts manuscript paragraphs, generates 3-8 review comments with suggestions, shows a diff preview of the injections, and (after approval) writes the environments and review blocks into the .tex file.
expectations: The output .tex file contains \newenvironment{review}
The output .tex file contains \newenvironment{addition-suggestion}
The output .tex file contains at least one \begin{review} block
The output .tex file contains at least one \begin{addition-suggestion} block
The skill asked for approval before writing (showed a diff or patch preview)
No full review text was printed as plain terminal output (it was injected into the file)

evals.prompt: Peer-review my paper without suggestions. Run /peer-review --file evals/fixtures/minimal.tex --no-suggest
evals.expected_output: The skill injects review blocks but NOT addition-suggestion blocks. The preamble still gets both environment definitions (they may be needed later). Review comments appear after relevant paragraphs.
expectations: The output .tex file contains at least one \begin{review} block
The output .tex file does NOT contain any \begin{addition-suggestion} block
The skill asked for approval before writing

evals.prompt: Review this manuscript which already has the environments defined. Run /peer-review --file evals/fixtures/full-preamble.tex
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that both environments are already defined and does NOT add duplicate definitions. It proceeds directly to extraction and review without patching the preamble. The final .tex has exactly one definition of each environment.
expectations: The output .tex file contains exactly one occurrence of \newenvironment{review}
The output .tex file contains exactly one occurrence of \newenvironment{addition-suggestion}
The output .tex file contains at least one \begin{review} block

evals.prompt: Do a dry-run peer review of my manuscript. Run /peer-review --file evals/fixtures/minimal.tex --dry-run
evals.expected_output: The skill prints a unified diff showing what would be inserted, but does NOT modify the .tex file. The original file content is unchanged after the run.
expectations: A unified diff is shown in the output (lines starting with + appear)
The original minimal.tex file is unchanged (its content does not contain \begin{review})

evals.prompt: Expert peer-review of my manuscript with project context. Run /peer-review --file evals/fixtures/expert-draft.tex --expert --project evals/fixtures/project-expert
evals.expected_output: The skill reads manuscript-context.md, loads methods/results context, and produces review comments that reference specific parameters (PBE-D3, 830 eV cutoff, RASPA2 GCMC, TraPPE-UA, counterpoise correction) rather than generic 'specify your DFT method'. Comments address known weaknesses (Al substitution, TraPPE-UA transferability, rigid framework) from manuscript-context.md. Review injected into .tex file.
expectations: The output .tex file contains at least one \begin{review} block
At least one review comment references a specific parameter from manuscript-context.md (e.g. PBE-D3, 830 eV, TraPPE-UA, RASPA2, counterpoise, or BSSE)
At least one review comment addresses a known weakness from manuscript-context.md (Al substitution, TraPPE-UA transferability, or rigid framework)
No warning about missing manuscript-context.md appears

evals.prompt: Expert peer-review without manuscript context. Run /peer-review --file evals/fixtures/expert-draft.tex --expert --project evals/fixtures/project-no-context
evals.expected_output: The skill finds no manuscript-context.md in the project dir, prints the fallback warning message telling the user to run /open-manuscript, falls back to memory files, and still produces a review (generic but functional).
expectations: A warning message appears mentioning 'manuscript-context.md not found' or 'Falling back'
The output .tex file contains at least one \begin{review} block
The warning recommends running /open-manuscript
